items.title: NAC heeft nog geen vervanger voor Van Hooijdonk, Van der Heide naar Portugal
items.summary: De winterse transfermarkt is geopend. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers in Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland.

items.title: Go Ahead Eagles-flop maakt miljoenentransfer naar koploper Championship
items.summary: Jahnoah Markelo (23) heeft een transfer van FC Zürich naar Coventry City afgerond. Beide clubs doen geen mededeling over het bedrag dat voor de Amsterdamse vleugelaanvaller wordt betaald, in de Zwitserse pers wordt gemeld dat de koploper van de Championship zo'n vijf miljoen euro overboekt.

items.title: Olympiakos-coach looft Ajax: 'Jonge spelers die doen wat er in ze opkomt'
items.summary: Olympiakos-trainer José Luis Mendilíbar heeft dinsdagavond vol lof gesproken over Ajax. De Spanjaard heeft een hoge pet op van met name de jeugdige onbevangenheid van de Amsterdamse club, zo meldde hij op zijn persconferentie daags voor de onderlinge Champions League-wedstrijd.

items.title: WSL2 minimum pay for under-23s less than national living wage for typical full-time job
items.summary: WSL says it is committed to increasing pay floors Clubs can be docked points for breaching salary cap Players aged under 23 in Women’s Super League 2 are not guaranteed to be paid the equivalent of the national living wage for a typical full-time worker annually, despite a large pay increase for the division’s lowest-paid players after the introduction of minimum salaries this season. WSL2 clubs must pay players aged 21 and 22 a minimum of £22,200 and those aged 18 to 20 at least £17,500. Regulations state they must receive a minimum “contact time” of 20 hours a week excluding matchdays and mealtimes. For players aged 23 and over, the minimum salary is £26,900. Continue reading...

items.title: Mou bids Arbeloa success -- except vs. Benfica
items.summary: Benfica coach José Mourinho wishes his former player Álvaro Arbeloa success as coach of Real Madrid except when they square off Wednesday in Lisbon in the Champions League.

items.title: Slot expects Robertson to stay at Liverpool
items.summary: Arne Slot has said he expects defender Andrew Robertson to stay at Liverpool for the rest of the season despite interest from Tottenham Hotspur.

items.title: 7 Greek fans die in crash en route to UEL game
items.summary: Seven fans of Greek soccer club PAOK Salonika died in a minibus crash in Romania while traveling to a Europa League game at Lyon, Greece's government confirmed Tuesday.

items.title: Dit kan Feyenoord verwachten van Real Betis
items.summary: Om de laatste kans op de Europa League-tussenronde te grijpen moet Feyenoord donderdagavond winnen bij Real Betis. Tactische analyse van de Spaanse ploeg die met veel blessures kampt, maar nog altijd herkenbaar voetbal speelt onder Manuel Pellegrini.

items.title: Conte slaat terug richting Spalletti: 'Hij moet oppassen wat hij zegt'
items.summary: Napoli-trainer Antonio Conte heeft gereageerd op de uitspraken van Juventus-trainer Luciano Spalletti na de topper in de Serie A ( 3-0 zege voor Juventus ). Spalletti refereerde aan Napoli als de voormalige kampioen van Italië, in plaats van de huidige kampioen.

items.title: Villa sign striker Abraham for £18.25m
items.summary: England striker Tammy Abraham completes an £18.25m return to Aston Villa from Turkish side Besiktas.

items.title: Abraham rejoins Aston Villa in £18.2m deal
items.summary: Aston Villa have signed striker Tammy Abraham from Besiktas in a deal worth £18.2m.

items.title: Celtic's appeal over Trusty red at Hearts dismissed
items.summary: Celtic's appeal over the red card shown to Auston Trusty in the draw at Hearts has been dismissed, following a Scottish FA hearing.

items.title: Hadjar crashes Red Bull after Hamilton makes first testing appearance
items.summary: Lewis Hamilton and Max Verstappen completed their first laps of the Barcelona shakedown before Isack Hadjar crashed his Red Bull on a rain-affected second day.

items.title: Arteta feeling bullish after Arsenal ‘take temperature down’ with team meeting
items.summary: Head coach and players spoke in wake of United defeat Side is ‘in great position in four competitions’, he says Mikel Arteta has revealed Arsenal held a team meeting after their defeat against Manchester United on Sunday “to take the temperature down”, insisting the league leaders must “play with enjoyment” in order to win a first Premier League title for 22 years. A late goal from Matheus Cunha inflicted on Arsenal their first home defeat of the season, with their lead at the top of the table remaining at four points after weekend victories for Manchester City and Aston Villa. Arteta’s side have finished as runners-up in the past three seasons and Arsenal have now spent 884 days at the summit since Arsène Wenger’s Invincibles were crowned champions in 2004. Continue reading...

items.title: The Premier League stars who could still move this transfer window
items.summary: There is less than a week left of the January transfer window. Here are the Premier League players who could relocate.

items.title: Transfer rumors, news: Cole Palmer open to Man United move
items.summary: Cole Palmer, who is reportedly homesick in London, is open to a move to Manchester United. Transfer Talk has the latest.

items.title: 'Jump on the fun boat' - Arteta appeals to Arsenal fans
items.summary: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta promises "beautiful" things that "we cannot even imagine" lie ahead after gathering his players together following their home defeat by Manchester United.

items.title: Newcastle latest: Joelinton out for weeks as Bruno faces fitness test
West Ham consider loaning Kinsky and Disasi as Bournemouth confirm Rayan deal
items.summary: Igor Julio must return to Brighton if West Ham are to act Tammy Abraham returns to Aston Villa in £18.25m deal West Ham are considering loan moves for Tottenham’s Antonin Kinsky and Chelsea’s Axel Disasi but will need to send Igor Julio back to Brighton if both moves are to go through. Nuno Espírito Santo has strengthened his attack by signing Taty Castellanos and Pablo Felipe for a package worth up to £47m and is looking to boost his side in their battle against relegation by bringing in a goalkeeper and a defender. Continue reading...

items.title: Arteta encouraged by 'beautiful' meeting with players in wake of Man Utd loss
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has revealed he called his players for a meeting to "bring down the temperature" - and pleaded with Arsenal's anxious fanbase to "jump on the fun boat" - in the wake of their defeat to Manchester United.
